{
  "reasoning": "The question asks for the oldest man who was elected president of the United States. To answer this, we must identify all U.S. presidents who were men and then determine their ages at the time of their election, focusing on the highest age.

Step 1: List all U.S. presidents who were men and note their birth dates and election years.

The U.S. presidents from 1789 to present include: Washington, Adams, Jefferson, Madison, Monroe, Adams, Jackson, Van Buren, Harrison, Tyler, Polk, Taylor, Fillmore, Pierce, Buchanan, Lincoln, Johnson, Grant, Hayes, Garfield, Arthur, Cleveland, Harrison, McKinley, Roosevelt, Taft, Wilson, Harding, Coolidge, Hoover, Roosevelt, Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, Bush Sr., Bush Jr., Clinton, Bush Jr., Obama, Trump, Biden.

Step 2: Identify the age of each man at the time of his election.

For example:
- Ronald Reagan was born on February 6, 1911. He was elected in 1980. He was 69 years old at the time of election.
- George H. W. Bush was born on June 12, 1924. He was elected in 1988. He was 64 years old.
- Donald Trump was born on June 14, 1946. He was elected in 2016. He was 70 years old.
- Barack Obama was born on August 4, 1961. He was elected in 2008. He was 47 years old.
- Joe Biden was born on November 20, 1942. He was elected in 2020. He was 77 years old.

Step 3: Check for the oldest man elected.

Joe Biden was born in 1942. In 2020, he was 77 years old when elected. This is the highest age among men elected as president.

No man elected president older than 77 years old.

Therefore, Joe Biden was the oldest man elected president of the United States at age 77.",
